metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students learn physical conditioning principles specific to dancers, with emphasis on injury prevention, strength development, and flexibility training. This course integrates anatomy, physiology, and movement science with practical conditioning exercises.
metadata.general_description: A course focused on physical conditioning, injury prevention, and strength development for dancers.

big_ideas.statement: Physical conditioning is essential to developing dancer strength, flexibility, and endurance while preventing injury.
big_ideas.elaboration: Dancers require specialized conditioning programs that address the unique physical demands of dance while building resilience and reducing risk of injury.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding anatomy and biomechanics informs effective and safe conditioning practices.
big_ideas.elaboration: Knowledge of skeletal, muscular, and connective tissue systems helps dancers make informed choices about conditioning exercises and movement patterns.

big_ideas.statement: Injury prevention requires awareness of individual physical needs, proper technique, and appropriate progression.
big_ideas.elaboration: Dancers develop self-awareness about their bodies, identify risk factors, and implement strategies to maintain long-term health and performance capacity.

big_ideas.statement: Comprehensive conditioning balances strength, flexibility, stability, and power development.
big_ideas.elaboration: Effective dance conditioning addresses multiple physical dimensions, creating well-rounded dancers capable of executing diverse movement vocabularies with control and artistry.

competencies.raw: Develop conditioning programs tailored to individual and dance-specific needs
i_can_statements: I can assess my own physical conditioning needs and identify areas for development
I can design conditioning exercises that address specific dance-related muscle groups
I can create balanced conditioning routines that develop multiple physical qualities
I can adapt conditioning exercises based on individual physical variations and limitations

competencies.raw: Practice diverse conditioning and strengthening exercises
i_can_statements: I can execute strength-building exercises with proper form and control
I can perform flexibility and stretching routines safely and effectively
I can engage in cardiovascular conditioning appropriate for dancers
I can explore innovative conditioning techniques and equipment

competencies.raw: Develop body awareness and proprioceptive control
i_can_statements: I can identify and contract specific muscles and muscle groups intentionally
I can develop awareness of body alignment and postural patterns
I can practice balance and proprioceptive exercises to improve stability
I can recognize how conditioning affects my movement quality and control

competencies.raw: Create personalized conditioning routines for home and studio practice
i_can_statements: I can design realistic conditioning routines for regular practice
I can modify exercises using available resources and equipment
I can establish sustainable conditioning habits and practices
I can track my physical development and progress over time

competencies.raw: Understand anatomy and biomechanics relevant to dance
i_can_statements: I can identify major skeletal, muscular, and connective tissue systems in the human body
I can explain how different muscle groups contribute to dance movements
I can understand the biomechanics of common dance movements
I can apply anatomical knowledge to conditioning exercise selection

competencies.raw: Recognize common dance injuries and injury prevention strategies
i_can_statements: I can identify risk factors and common injuries in dance
I can explain how specific conditioning practices prevent injuries
I can understand proper progression and load management
I can apply injury prevention principles to my own conditioning practice

competencies.raw: Analyze the relationship between conditioning and dance performance
i_can_statements: I can evaluate how conditioning affects my technical abilities and artistry
I can reflect on how physical strength and flexibility enhance my movement quality
I can identify how conditioning addresses specific dance technique challenges
I can recognize the connection between physical wellness and performance readiness

competencies.raw: Develop critical evaluation skills regarding conditioning information and practices
i_can_statements: I can evaluate the safety and effectiveness of conditioning methods
I can assess conditioning advice from diverse sources for validity and appropriateness
I can distinguish between evidence-based and unfounded conditioning claims
I can make informed decisions about my conditioning practices

competencies.raw: Document personal conditioning progress and physical development
i_can_statements: I can maintain records of conditioning routines and exercises
I can track physical measurements, flexibility, and strength improvements
I can document pain, discomfort, or physical concerns
I can use progress documentation to adjust and refine my conditioning programs

competencies.raw: Communicate with instructors and health professionals about conditioning
i_can_statements: I can describe my conditioning practices and physical concerns to instructors
I can ask appropriate questions about safe conditioning practices
I can seek professional advice from physical therapists or conditioning specialists
I can report pain, injury, or physical limitations promptly and clearly

competencies.raw: Share conditioning knowledge and support peer health and wellness
i_can_statements: I can explain conditioning exercises to other dancers
I can provide peer support and encouragement for conditioning practice
I can share evidence-based conditioning information responsibly
I can advocate for healthy conditioning practices within dance communities

competencies.raw: Articulate connections between conditioning practices and personal health
i_can_statements: I can explain how my conditioning affects my overall health and wellbeing
I can discuss the importance of physical wellness to my dance career
I can communicate my personal conditioning goals and progress
I can reflect on my developing relationship with my body through conditioning

competencies.raw: Connect conditioning practices to diverse dance styles and movement demands
i_can_statements: I can understand how conditioning needs vary across different dance styles
I can adapt conditioning for specific choreographic demands
I can develop conditioning protocols for different performance contexts
I can recognize how conditioning supports diverse artistic and technical goals

competencies.raw: Explore advanced conditioning methods and emerging practices
i_can_statements: I can experiment with innovative conditioning techniques and approaches
I can research and evaluate new conditioning methods and technologies
I can integrate cross-training and complementary movement practices
I can develop personal conditioning practices informed by contemporary science

competencies.raw: Connect conditioning to broader health, wellness, and longevity
i_can_statements: I can understand how conditioning supports long-term career sustainability
I can recognize the importance of conditioning for overall health and quality of life
I can develop habits that support lifelong physical wellness
I can integrate conditioning with nutrition, rest, and stress management

competencies.raw: Engage with conditioning in educational and professional contexts
i_can_statements: I can learn from professional dancers and conditioning specialists
I can understand evidence-based conditioning practices in professional dance
I can explore career pathways in dance conditioning and training
I can contribute to conditioning culture that prioritizes dancer health and wellness

areas.name: Anatomy and Biomechanics
topics: Skeletal system and joint structure relevant to dance
Muscular system and muscle function in movement
Connective tissues: tendons, ligaments, and fascia
Individual anatomical variations and their implications

areas.name: Strength and Power Development
topics: Lower body strengthening for jump capacity and control
Upper body strength for support and expression

areas.name: Injury Prevention and Rehabilitation
topics: Common dance injuries and prevention strategies
Risk assessment and individual risk factors
Recognition of pain and warning signs
Working with healthcare professionals and prevention protocols
